commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Setim-wood. The wood of a tree that grows in the wilderness, which is said to be incorruptible, (Challoner) as the Septuagint intimate. It is perhaps the Acacia, which is very black and hard. St. Jerome in Joel iii. 18, says it resembles our white thorn.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Setim wood: The wood of a tree that grows in the wilderness, which is said to be incorruptible.
